title: trifolium pratense var. sativum
intro: Trifolium pratense var. sativum, commonly known as red clover, is a legume plant that belongs to the pea family and is cultivated for its leaves and flowers. It contains isoflavones, particularly coumarin and formononetin, which possess medicinal properties, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects. The health benefits of red clover include reducing menopausal symptoms, alleviating osteoporosis, and potentially lowering cholesterol levels, due to its antioxidant and estrogen-like properties. Red clover is often prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in topical creams and ointments for skin conditions, such as eczema and acne.
